steps.title: Find angle C in triangle ABC using angle sum
steps.content: Angle $A = 27^{\circ}$ and angle $B = 90^{\circ}$ are given. Angles in a triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$, so: $$C = 180^{\circ} - 27^{\circ} - 90^{\circ} = 63^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Use congruence to find angle F
steps.content:
The congruence specifies that $A$ corresponds to $D$. Since angle $B = 90^{\circ}$ and angle $E = 90^{\circ}$, vertex $B$ corresponds to vertex $E$. Therefore, vertex $C$ corresponds to vertex $F$.
In congruent triangles, corresponding angles are equal, so angle $F = 63^{\circ}$.
